title: One Artery Feeds Every Organ: Trunks That Split Late
strategies: star baseline: wire every organ straight to the arterial source (minimizes delivery, pays full Murray material per organ; == checker baseline B, ~0.1)

nearest-reach growth: grow the tree by repeatedly attaching each organ to its nearest already-connected node via an obstacle-free segment (space-colonization greedy) -- chains organs, aggregates a little but zig-zags

shared-trunk aggregation: cluster organs by direction, run one thick trunk from the source to a Weiszfeld-optimal junction per cluster and fan out late (Murray cube law makes material concave in flow, so a shared trunk over the long haul is far cheaper); route around obstacles

reformulation: total cost = sum over edges of len*(Wm*flow^(2/3) + Wd*flow) with flow = downstream demand; a weighted geometric-median (Steiner) placement of junctions minimizes it for a fixed aggregation topology
